jokes.text: When did you date Sebastien Raine? / They dated for like a month and a half and David got very upset about it. / Okay, It was almost three months. Four, if you include the month that he was seeing other people.
jokes.setup: Simple question about when David dated Sebastien
jokes.punchline: David keeps extending the timeline, including the month Sebastien was seeing other people
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing David's tendency to over-explain and make himself the victim. The escalation is beautifully structured.

jokes.text: He's a monster, who uses people, and leaves them for dead.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Sebastien Raine's professional reputation
jokes.punchline: David's dramatically over-the-top description reveals his personal hurt
jokes.explanation: Classic David melodrama - treating a breakup like a war crime. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Okay, It was almost three months. Four, if you include the month that he was seeing other people.
jokes.setup: David corrects the timeline to defend himself
jokes.punchline: Correction only makes it worse by revealing infidelity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - David trying to save face only digs himself deeper. The math makes the humiliation precise.

jokes.text: Remember when he dumped you? And you ate all those mall pretzels, and watched 'Bridget Jones's Diary' every day for a year. / It wasn't a whole year, And I will not feel shame about the mall pretzels.
jokes.setup: Alexis recounting David's post-breakup behavior
jokes.punchline: David's defensive correction and dignified stand about mall pretzels
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - the specific details (mall pretzels, Bridget Jones) feel authentic and David's defense is perfectly him.

jokes.text: I could cancel, but you just told me you don't care so now I have no choice but to meet him.
jokes.setup: David says he doesn't care about the Sebastien visit
jokes.punchline: Moira's twisted logic that his indifference forces her to proceed
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira logic - turning David's attempt at indifference into permission for her own desires.

jokes.text: He's most likely wearing a very expensive sweater, that doesn't look very expensive. / Is he like, really... Really handsome in a homeless-y way sort of way? Yes.
jokes.setup: David describing Sebastien's appearance to help Stevie identify him
jokes.punchline: Stevie's perfectly crafted description: 'handsome in a homeless-y way'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures a specific aesthetic. 'Homeless-y way' is comedically perfect.

jokes.text: He's walking. / He's walking. / He's walking towards here. / Where is he walking to? / And now he's coming in / Now he's coming in... and so he's here. / He's here.
jokes.setup: David asking Stevie to spy on Sebastien's approach
jokes.punchline: The increasingly frantic play-by-play commentary building to anticlimax
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of nervous energy, though the repetition runs a bit long.

jokes.text: This is... exactly how I pictured it.
jokes.setup: Sebastien entering the shabby motel room
jokes.punchline: His 'compliment' is clearly an insult about their reduced circumstances
jokes.explanation: Effective condescending comment that establishes Sebastien's character as pretentious.

jokes.text: Look at you... You look really... Healthy.
jokes.setup: Sebastien seeing David after their breakup
jokes.punchline: The pause before 'healthy' - clearly not the compliment he intended
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward beat. The pause suggests he was searching for something nicer to say.

jokes.text: I think you're brave.
jokes.setup: Sebastien looking around at their living situation
jokes.punchline: Calling their poverty 'brave' - backhanded condescension
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates pretentious artist speak - turning their circumstances into performance art.

jokes.text: You must be David's girlfriend. / No... / No, I own The Motel, so that makes me more like your landlord? / No. It doesn't.
jokes.setup: Sebastien assumes Stevie is David's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Stevie's logic about being landlord, David's flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Great character interaction showing Stevie's deflection and David's matter-of-fact correction.

jokes.text: I'd love to Polaroid you naked one day. / Okay. / Okay?!
jokes.setup: Sebastien making an inappropriate proposition to Stevie
jokes.punchline: Stevie's casual 'okay' and David's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Stevie's unflappable response vs David's horror at his ex hitting on his friend.

jokes.text: So brave. / So brave.
jokes.setup: Sebastien's earlier 'brave' comment
jokes.punchline: He keeps repeating his condescending catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Establishes his pretentious verbal tic, though repetition feels a bit forced.

jokes.text: I love it. It's just so unassertive. You can't find this in New York City.
jokes.setup: Sebastien looking around the cafe
jokes.punchline: Calling a shabby small-town cafe 'unassertive' as if it's an artistic choice
jokes.explanation: Great word choice - 'unassertive' perfectly captures pretentious art speak.

jokes.text: Creativity lives on its feet. Anything else is bullshit. / I just ordered you a Sanka-ccino.
jokes.setup: Sebastien's pretentious statement about creativity requiring movement
jokes.punchline: Moira's mundane coffee order immediately undercuts his philosophy
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between artistic pomposity and coffee shop reality.

jokes.text: But, I volunteer to work here. / Because it's your job.
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to claim volunteer hours for her paid job
jokes.punchline: Ted's matter-of-fact correction of her illogical reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis logic - trying to game the system with nonsensical reasoning.

jokes.text: I thought my community service hours would apply but they don't count because their court-ordered.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her volunteer hour shortage
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of court-ordered community service
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis throwaway line revealing her checkered past.

jokes.text: Um, it's just I have a thing with skin tags and old smells. So I might just need to think on it. / Well, tonight's gonna be fun though, dance lessons! / We'd have to touch them.
jokes.setup: Alexis expressing reservations about working with seniors
jokes.punchline: Her horror at the prospect of physical contact during dance lessons
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's superficial concerns, though a bit mean-spirited.

jokes.text: Now act like you're talking to me. / I am talking to you. / No. Not like that. It's got to be more conversational.
jokes.setup: Roland asking Johnny to act natural
jokes.punchline: Roland's critique of Johnny's actual conversation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - Roland directing a conversation about having a conversation.

jokes.text: Tell him that we're doing something together tonight, okay? Tell him... you're taking me for a haircut tonight. / Haircut?
jokes.setup: Roland asking Johnny to lie about their evening plans
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous specificity of a nighttime haircut appointment
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of the timing makes it funny, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: I wouldn't mind playing, Bob. / Really?
jokes.setup: Johnny supposed to help Roland avoid poker
jokes.punchline: Johnny immediately agreeing to play, betraying Roland's plan
jokes.explanation: Good betrayal moment, though the setup was obvious.

jokes.text: Can I ask you something? Um, do you have even the slightest concept of what just happened here?
jokes.setup: Johnny completely missing Roland's scheme
jokes.punchline: Roland's exasperated disbelief at Johnny's obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Good character payoff showing the dynamic between Roland and Johnny.

jokes.text: At the motel? And here I thought I gave you very specific instructions to go directly to the cafe. / Ever the renegade.
jokes.setup: Moira discovering Sebastien got a motel room
jokes.punchline: Calling going to a motel instead of cafe 'renegade' behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary - making mundane actions sound dramatic.

jokes.text: You must have used our name.
jokes.setup: Sebastien getting a room at the usually booked motel
jokes.punchline: Moira's assumption about their non-existent influence
jokes.explanation: Good delusion of grandeur moment for Moira

jokes.text: You must have used our name. / But Sebastien, I was under the impression that you were here just for today for a quick consult...
jokes.setup: Moira realizing Sebastien got a room easily
jokes.punchline: Her growing concern that this wasn't supposed to be an overnight visit
jokes.explanation: Shows Moira realizing she's losing control of the situation.

jokes.text: This is my talk now, shoot later look. So, this visage is off-limits for-for the moment.
jokes.setup: Sebastien trying to take photos of Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's elaborate way of saying 'don't photograph me right now'
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - using 'visage' and creating elaborate phrases for simple concepts.

jokes.text: There's a provincial romanticism to all this, that's just... terrifying and important.
jokes.setup: Sebastien looking at the rural landscape
jokes.punchline: Describing rural scenery as 'terrifying and important' with pretentious art speak
jokes.explanation: Perfect pretentious artist dialogue - making normal countryside sound profound.

jokes.text: Let the field reveal itself to you.
jokes.setup: Sebastien directing Moira for photos
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-mystical direction about connecting with a field
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures pretentious photographer speak - mystical nonsense.

jokes.text: picturettes, really.
jokes.setup: Moira describing the photos Sebastien took
jokes.punchline: Making up the word 'picturettes' to minimize their importance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira word creation - the diminutive suffix makes it both pretentious and dismissive.

jokes.text: I leaned in! I don't know, David. Why don't you try it... sometime!
jokes.setup: David criticizing Moira for being photographed
jokes.punchline: Moira's defensive suggestion that David should try 'leaning in'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira turning self-help speak into an insult.

jokes.text: Dot, you won't need the Swiffer. / I guess I'll be dancing with Ted.
jokes.setup: Ted organizing dance partners for seniors
jokes.punchline: Dot apparently brought cleaning supplies to dance class
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag about elderly person's confusion at dance class.

jokes.text: Although I did think that there would be more of them. / Yeah, well there used to be, but... you know.
jokes.setup: Alexis commenting on the small group of seniors
jokes.punchline: Ted's trailing off implying the others died
jokes.explanation: Dark humor handled with appropriate delicacy through implication.

jokes.text: Oh, okay, but these ones actually smell like very clean, like baby powder.
jokes.setup: Alexis's earlier concern about 'old smells'
jokes.punchline: Her relief that the seniors smell acceptable to her
jokes.explanation: Callback to her earlier shallowness, though the humor feels a bit mean.

jokes.text: Does Dot have arthritis? Because it looks like she's giving me the finger. / Well, she has arthritis, but not in that hand.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about Dot's hand gesture
jokes.punchline: Ted confirming Dot is indeed giving her the finger
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and punchline structure. Ted's deadpan delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: Well, she has arthritis, but not in that hand.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about Dot's arthritis
jokes.punchline: Ted confirming Dot IS giving her the finger
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - we expect defense of the elderly lady but Ted confirms she's being rude. Economy and timing are excellent.

jokes.text: Full house. Read 'em and weep. / And I think I come in second because I got a pair of Queens. / There's no second place, Roland.
jokes.setup: Poker game results being announced
jokes.punchline: Roland's fundamental misunderstanding of poker
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland incompetence - not understanding basic game rules.

jokes.text: And I think I come in second because I got a pair of Queens. There's no second place, Roland.
jokes.setup: Roland thinks he understands poker rankings
jokes.punchline: Fundamental misunderstanding of how poker works
jokes.explanation: Roland's complete incompetence at poker is in character but the joke is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Yeah, I guess it's another trip to Bob's laundromat, right? You know, because he keeps taking us to the cleaners.
jokes.setup: Roland making a pun about losing money
jokes.punchline: Connecting Bob's laundromat to 'taking them to the cleaners'
jokes.explanation: Obvious pun that feels forced, though it fits Roland's character.

jokes.text: But ah, I do miss my wristwatch.
jokes.setup: Johnny discussing his poker losses
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of losing his watch to gambling
jokes.explanation: Good understated reveal of how much Johnny has lost gambling.

jokes.text: Ahhh. Like if she had an 'accident' or something? / I don't mean bump her off, Roland.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting they get Gwen out of the room
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately jumping to murder
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Roland's literal-mindedness taken to dark extremes.

jokes.text: Kosher? / And halal. / Gwen, you called it!
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to think of dietary restrictions to send Gwen shopping
jokes.punchline: Roland adding more restrictions and Bob's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of their desperate excuses, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: I don't think you want to drive, Roland. / I don't mind. I love driving. / I don't think you do. / Yeah, I don't love driving. Not at night. No! / And I'm drunk.
jokes.setup: Roland offering to drive to the store
jokes.punchline: The escalating reasons why he shouldn't drive, ending with being drunk
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect timing - saves the best reason for last.

jokes.text: The photos are really haunting. / Haunting. / Sure she'd be thrilled about that adjective.
jokes.setup: Sebastien describing his photos of Moira as 'haunting'
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic response about how Moira would feel about that description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - David protecting his mother while being sarcastic.

jokes.text: And while my therapist said I should never feel sorrow, I do appreciate your pain.
jokes.setup: Sebastien attempting to apologize to David
jokes.punchline: The pretentious therapy-speak non-apology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - perfectly captures narcissistic therapy-speak as non-apology.

jokes.text: Dear, we know all about the failed engagements.
jokes.setup: Conversation about Alexis and Ted's relationship
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Alexis's multiple failed engagements
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Alexis's romantic history, delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Is she not cute? / She's not you.
jokes.setup: Alexis asking about Joan's granddaughter
jokes.punchline: Joan's romantic assessment of why Ted isn't interested
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and delivery - Joan being unexpectedly romantic.

jokes.text: My husband and I were really close friends for 52 years.
jokes.setup: Alexis insisting she and Ted are just friends
jokes.punchline: Joan's wise comment about her marriage being friendship
jokes.explanation: Sweet and wise - Joan turning Alexis's excuse into marriage advice.

jokes.text: I would rather dance with the Swiffer.
jokes.setup: Alexis offering to dance with Dot
jokes.punchline: Dot's brutal rejection preferring cleaning equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and perfect senior sass - preferring a mop to Alexis.

jokes.text: Well, Uh... You thought we were cheating? / No! No... I / No offense, but I don't need to cheat to beat any of you.
jokes.setup: Johnny forced to explain his cheating theory
jokes.punchline: Bob's matter-of-fact dismissal that he doesn't need to cheat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bob's confidence and the group's poor poker skills.

jokes.text: And Roland... well, you know... I don't even know if you know the rules. / I don't know all of them. No.
jokes.setup: Bob explaining everyone's poker tells
jokes.punchline: Roland's honest admission about not knowing the rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland moment - his honesty about incompetence is endearing.

jokes.text: Oh, I'd love to believe you, but there's a big fat line between charm and bullshit.
jokes.setup: Sebastien trying to convince Moira about his artistic vision
jokes.punchline: Moira's blunt assessment of his manipulation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira moment - using colorful language to call out manipulation.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'd give it back, but I don't think it'll be of much use to you. It accidentally fell out of your camera into my hand last night. And then fell out of my hand into my drink. And then I stepped on it a lot.
jokes.setup: David revealing he has Sebastien's memory card
jokes.punchline: The escalating 'accidents' that destroyed the photos
jokes.explanation: Perfect David triumph moment - the escalating 'accidents' are clearly deliberate and satisfying.

jokes.text: It wasn't just for you. Let's just say we both won.
jokes.setup: Moira thanking David for protecting her
jokes.punchline: David acknowledging he got revenge on his ex while helping his mother
jokes.explanation: Nice resolution showing David's character growth - helping family while getting revenge.

jokes.text: Those aren't for you. / How's that watch working out for you? / I love it. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Johnny reaching for food at the poker game
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Bob has Johnny's watch from gambling losses
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the extent of Johnny's gambling losses.
